title: Equal COLA Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
plain_summary: The Equal COLA Act changes how much extra money retired federal workers get each year to help with rising costs. Right now, workers who retired under the newer system get smaller yearly increases than workers who retired under the older system. This bill makes it so both groups get the same cost-of-living increases that match exactly how much prices have gone up. This means retired federal workers will get fair increases to help them afford things as they get more expensive.
last_action_text: ASSUMING FIRST SPONSORSHIP - Mr. Walkinshaw asked unanimous consent that he may hereafter be considered as the first sponsor of H.R. 491, a bill originally introduced by Representative Connolly, for t
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Equal COLA Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Increase in COLA for FERS Annuities
sections.text: This section changes how cost-of-living adjustments work for federal employees who retire under the Federal Employees Retirement System (also called FERS). Currently, FERS retirees get smaller cost-of-living increases than people who retired under the older Civil Service Retirement System. Starting on December first of each year, FERS retirement payments will be increased by the same amount as the change in the Consumer Price Index from the previous year. This increase will be rounded to the nearest one-tenth of one percent. These changes will apply to all cost-of-living adjustments made after this law is passed. It will also apply to all retirement payments under FERS, whether those payments started before, on, or after this law becomes effective.
